tagline: Precise instruction-follower — give explicit steps + a stop condition.
effort_line: Give explicit, itemized steps and a numeric effort/step budget with a concrete stop condition. Sonnet 5 follows precise instructions better than it infers, so spell out the desired behavior.
idioms: Give trigger → action example pairs, not just abstract rules.
State a numeric step/effort budget and an explicit stop condition for autonomous work.
notes: Mid-tier workhorse: fast, cost-efficient, optimized for precise instruction-following rather than open-ended inference. Needs more explicit, itemized steering than Opus: numbered decision steps with per-step stop conditions and concrete trigger->action examples. Let it act on reasonable defaults and state assumptions in one line; for any multi-step work, give a numeric budget and a checkable completion condition, since it doesn't sustain open-ended judgment as gracefully as Opus without one.
